code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test18-MAY-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly with tubular dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes such as diabetic or non-diabetic kidney disease.
